title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the launch angle, we need to consider the horizontal and vertical components of the motion. The horizontal distance covered is $$69.0$$ $$m$$, and the initial speed is $$35.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Breaking Down the Motion
text: The horizontal component of the velocity can be expressed as \(v_x=v*cos(\theta)\), where \(v\) is the initial speed and \(\theta\) is the launch angle.

title: Time of Flight
text: The time of flight can be calculated using the horizontal motion equation: \(d=v_x*t\), where \(d\) is the horizontal distance.

title: Vertical Motion Equation
text: The vertical component of the velocity is \(v_y=v*sin(\theta)\). Use the vertical motion equation \(y=v_y*t-0.5*g*t**2\) to find the time of flight.

title: Calculate the Launch Angle
text: Using the equations for horizontal and vertical motion, solve for the launch angle \(\theta\).
